reference_scope: aMule/eMule ClientTCPSocket, ListenSocket, BaseClient; MLDonkey defaults; Wireshark eMule tags
target_behavior: Aligned hello and hello answer capability construction through one local peer-info helper. Standalone eMule info keeps reference-compatible legacy feature tags, while hello misc-options carry modern AICH, Unicode, compression, SX1, SX2, and large-file claims. Multipacket, extended multipacket, crypt, secure-ident, Kad, and file-ident claims remain unadvertised until matching packet/state paths exist.
notes: Truthful capability claims must precede request-flow work.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule DownloadClient and ClientTCPSocket; Wireshark multipacket; protocol docs
target_behavior: Plain fallback request sequencing is aligned: OP_REQUESTFILENAME carries hash plus supported extended metadata, multi-part files use OP_SETREQFILEID for OP_FILESTATUS, OP_HASHSETREQUEST and OP_HASHSETANSWER use the reference hash+count+hashes shape, Source Exchange and AICH requests are separate eMule packets, upload starts through OP_STARTUPLOADREQ, queue/accept/no-file responses are handled, and shared responders return matching filename/status/hashset/AICH/source/no-file packets. Multipacket, extended multipacket, OP_MULTIPACKET_EXT2, OP_HASHSETREQUEST2, OP_HASHSETANSWER2, and file identifiers remain unadvertised and disabled until a later checkpoint implements the complete send/receive/state/fallback surface.
verification: Focused aMule/eMule/Wireshark/protocol-doc comparison and current packet/state inspection; no code change required.

checkpoint: Peer transfer control and failure state
reference_scope: aMule/eMule transfer state machines; MLDonkey peer flow; protocol docs
target_behavior: Align request parts, I64 request parts, sending parts, compressed parts, cancel, out-of-parts, no-file, disconnect, duplicate rejection, retry scoring, backoff, requested-range cleanup, and queue state.
notes: Disk and integrity details close in RA40.
